channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former Colorado sheriff’s deputy convicted of homicide in shooting death of man in crisis
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A former Colorado sheriff’s deputy was convicted Thursday in the shooting death of a 22-year-old man in distress who called 911 for help after his car got stuck in a small mountain community.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump holds Black History Month event as some agencies skip recognition after anti-DEI order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump marked Black History Month at the White House on Thursday, preserving a tradition by holding a reception at the same time that his executive order ending the federal government's diversity, equity and inclusion programs has disrupted its observance elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Once off the table, bills to charge women who get abortions with murder get votes before failing
item.description: Abortion rights groups fear punishment for women who have abortions and governments tracking pregnancies.
item.dc:creator: Kimberlee Kruesi And Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drake suprises pregnant fan with VIP tickets and $30,000
item.description: Drake gifted one fan in a major way during a Sydney show on his Anita Max Win Tour in Australia on Wednesday night. A pregnant fan, identified as Tiana Henderson, was sitting in the pit holding a sign that read "I'm 20 weeks pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Funeral held for 2 Detroit kids who died in cold while family gets promise of a home
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A group has stepped forward to provide a home for surviving family members of two Detroit children who died in a cold van in a casino parking garage, an official said. A funeral was held Thursday for Darnell Currie Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bird flu vaccine for chickens given conditional license from USDA
item.description: The company, Zoetis, said this license was granted on the “demonstration of safety, purity, and reasonable expectation of efficacy.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Wendy's plans to add more AI ordering assistants to restaurants nationwide
item.description: Wendy’s plans to expand its “voice-enabled AI order taking" at up to 600 restaurants nationwide by the end of the year, CEO Kirk Tanner confirmed during a recent earnings call. The burger chain currently uses its AI voice assistant, which it began testing in 2023, in around 100 Wendy’s locations.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Mitch McConnell announced on Thursday that he won’t seek reelection next year, ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce GOP populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Senate confirms Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Kash Patel to serve as the head of the FBI, approving a pick with a string of controversial statements who has been accused by Democrats of directing a purge of bureau employees. Patel was confirmed in a 51-49 vote. Two Republicans, Sens.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Officials investigating bomb threat at Kennedy Center, police say
item.description: The Kennedy Center was evacuated on Thursday after a bomb threat was reported at the venue, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Odyssey Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 students arrested for allegedly plotting 'mass casualty attack' at Houston school, FBI says
item.description: Two high school students have been arrested for planning what authorities are calling a “mass casualty attack” at a Houston high school.
item.dc:creator: Chad Washington, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: CBS News anchor's 'incredibly dumb' take on the Holocaust
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black & Decker sues Stanley cup maker over use of name
item.description: The Connecticut-based Stanley Black & Decker is suing the company behind the popular drinkware, Stanley, over the use of its name.
item.dc:creator: Jareliz Diaz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration nixes legal representation for thousands of migrant children
item.description: President Trump has made cracking down on immigration a key tenet of his second term, including in some cases the elimination of certain government services.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump approval rating appears to be underwater one month in: Survey
item.description: The CNN poll shows that while 47 percent of adults in the U.S. say they approve of Trump's moves since returning to the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Arkansas victims of mortuary theft scheme inspire push for law banning sale of human remains
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A year had passed since Doneysha Smith suffered a stillbirth and the memories of her loss lingered in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New, cheaper iPhone 16e has 'best battery life ever,' Apple says
item.description: "iPhone 16e has the best battery life ever on a 6.1-inch iPhone, lasting up to six hours longer than iPhone 11 and up to 12 hours longer than all generations of iPhone SE," Apple said.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: NASA looking for ways to destroy asteroid that could strike Earth, kill city
item.description: NASA and other space agencies are attempting to come up with a way to destroy a massive asteroid unlikely to strike Earth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, WGN 9

item.title: IRS plans to lay off 7,000 probationary workers beginning Thursday, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS will lay off roughly 7,000 workers in Washington and around the country beginning Thursday, a person familiar with the plans told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 things to watch at this year's CPAC
item.description: Conservatives are gathering in National Harbor, Md., this week for the annual Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in what is poised to be a celebration for the political right following President Trump’s election victory in November.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: DHS fires roughly 400 probationary employees
item.description: The firings came after the Office of Personnel Management directed agencies to fire probationary employees, typically those who have been on the job for up to one year.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KTLA 5

item.title: Kennedy Center cancels Pride performance featuring Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington
item.description: The Kennedy Center canceled an upcoming performance set to feature the Gay Men’s Chorus of Washington, which the center said was due to financial constraints and scheduling conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man who admitted to shooting Black teen at wrong door dies before sentencing
item.description: Andrew Lester, a man who pleaded guilty last week to second-degree assault after he shot Ralph Yarl, an unarmed teenage boy in Kansas City in 2023, has died, according to a Clay County prosecutor.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aircrafts collide in midair near Arizona airport; 2 reported dead
item.description: The NTSB said one of the airplanes landed "uneventfully" after the collision, while the other crashed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Trails, bathrooms, wait times: How National Park staffing cuts could impact your next visit
item.description: While thousands of seasonal staff are expected to be hired, the loss of other employees could make your next national park visit feel different.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Fertility clinic sued after giving woman the wrong IVF baby
item.description: A former patient of Coastal Fertility Specialists has filed a lawsuit against the company for implanting the wrong embryo into her uterus, resulting in her carrying and giving birth to another couple’s biological child.
item.dc:creator: Dylan James, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump will sign new executive orders while his first joint TV interview with Musk airs in prime time
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday was set to sign new executive orders from his Florida home while his first joint TV interview with adviser Elon Musk airs in prime time.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Netflix series brings new attention to Gabby Petito case
item.description: Gabby's family and friends gave interviews for the three-part Netflix series called "American Murder: Gabby Petito.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What is a polar vortex? US gets a taste of the Arctic this week
item.description: The term “polar vortex” usually refers to the gigantic circular upper air weather pattern in the Arctic region. It is a normal pattern that is stronger in the winter and keeps some of the coldest weather bottled up near the North Pole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4
